title: erysimum helveticum subsp. rhaeticum
intro: Erysimum helveticum subsp. rhaeticum, also known as the alpine wallflower or Rhaetic wallflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and fight off infections. The health benefits of Erysimum helveticum subsp. rhaeticum include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of ways.
